name: Thomas Mann
description: German novelist and Nobel Prize laureate (1875–1955)
abstract: Paul Thomas Mann (6 June 1875 – 12 August 1955) was a German novelist, short story writer, social critic, philanthropist, essayist, and the 1929 Nobel Prize in Literature laureate. His highly symbolic and ironic epic novels and novellas are noted for their insight into the psychology of the artist and the intellectual.

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Murray
about_quotes.evidence: what I was thinking about was Thomas Mann had a house at Princeton and there was all kinds of guys at Princeton. So I associated with that
about_quotes.doc_title: Albert Murray interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 24, 1997
